site.name: Ổn Mà, Từ Từ
site.description: Short motivational animation stories about mindset, growth, and being kind to yourself — it's okay, take it slow.

affirmations.title: Daily Affirmations
affirmations.description: A collection of gentle morning reminders from {siteName}.
affirmations.intro: Pick how you're feeling, or see all of them.

about.title: About
about.description: The story behind {siteName} and its companion character.
about.heading: About {siteName}
about.charAlt: The companion character of Ổn Mà, Từ Từ
about.p1: is a short animation channel for people who are working, studying, and sometimes feel like everything is a bit too much.
about.p2: The main character isn't a "motivational coach" — more like a friend sitting next to you, saying: "it's okay, take it slow, you'll be fine".
about.p3: Each video covers a small, everyday situation — failure, self-comparison, exhaustion, burnout — with a positive perspective that isn't preachy or clichéd.
about.p4: Follow the channel on TikTok, YouTube Shorts, Facebook & Instagram Reels so you don't miss a new story each week.

shareStory.title: Share your story
shareStory.subtitle: No one knows who you are. Just write.
shareStory.metaDescription: Share a painful story, a sadness, or something weighing on your heart — completely anonymous, no one knows who you are.
shareStory.notice: This is a space to let it out.
shareStory.noticeDetail: Your story is completely anonymous — we don't collect your name, email, or any information about you.
shareStory.guideHeading: You don't need to write perfectly — just talk like you're texting a friend.
guideItems: What happened? (the more specific the better — time, place, situation)
How did you feel in that moment?
What's weighing on you the most right now?
shareStory.guideOptional: Optional: was there a moment that made you see things differently?
shareStory.placeholder: e.g. Today on the way home from work, my boss called me in and said...
shareStory.successMessage: We read every story. You are not alone.
shareStory.errorMessage: Something went wrong. Please try again later.
shareStory.minLength: Your story needs to be at least 10 characters.
shareStory.footer: Only we will read this. No one else.

breathingTool.title: Breathing exercise
breathingTool.metaDescription: An interactive breathing tool — box breathing, 4-7-8, or coherent breathing — to help you feel less stressed.
breathingTool.intro: Pick a breathing pattern, hit start, and follow the circle.
breathingTool.reducedMotionNotice: Your device has reduced motion enabled — the circle won't scale, only text and a countdown will show.
breathingTool.disclaimer: This tool doesn't replace medical advice. If you're dealing with ongoing anxiety, please reach out to a mental health professional.
breathingTool.disclaimerLinkLabel: Read more about breathing exercises for stress

box.label: Box breathing
box.description: Breathe in 4s, hold 4s, breathe out 4s, hold 4s — repeat steadily.

478.description: Breathe in 4s, hold 7s, breathe out 8s — good for winding down before sleep.

coherent.label: Coherent breathing
coherent.description: Breathe in 5s, breathe out 5s — an even rhythm that's easy to sustain.

bigFive.title: Big Five Personality Test
bigFive.metaDescription: Discover your 5 core personality traits with the Big Five (IPIP-NEO) test — free, right in your browser, no sign-up needed.

intro.heading: Big Five Personality Test
intro.body: The Big Five is the most widely researched personality model in psychology, measuring 5 core traits: Openness, Conscientiousness, Extraversion, Agreeableness, and Emotional Stability. This test has 60 questions and takes about 8-10 minutes.
intro.disclaimer: This is a self-discovery tool for reference only, not a medical or psychological diagnostic instrument. Your results reflect tendencies, not a fixed conclusion about who you are.

openness.name: Openness
openness.highBlurb: You tend to be curious, drawn to new ideas, and excited by unfamiliar experiences. You're often drawn to art, different perspectives, and change.
openness.lowBlurb: You tend to prefer familiarity, practicality, and stability over novelty. You feel most at ease when things are clear, concrete, and predictable.

conscientiousness.name: Conscientiousness
conscientiousness.highBlurb: You tend to be organized, disciplined, and reliable at work. You usually plan ahead and try to follow through carefully on what you start.
conscientiousness.lowBlurb: You tend to be flexible and easygoing rather than tied to rigid plans. You're comfortable letting things unfold naturally instead of tightly controlling them.

extraversion.name: Extraversion
extraversion.highBlurb: You tend to feel energized around other people, enjoy socializing, and feel comfortable being the center of attention.
extraversion.lowBlurb: You tend to recharge by spending time alone and prefer deeper one-on-one conversations over large, noisy gatherings.

agreeableness.name: Agreeableness
agreeableness.highBlurb: You tend to be caring, empathetic, and quick to help others. You usually prioritize harmony in your relationships.
agreeableness.lowBlurb: You tend to be direct, independent, and confident in your own views. You don't shy away from saying what you think, even when it's not easy to hear.

neuroticism.name: Emotional Stability
neuroticism.highBlurb: You tend to be sensitive to stress and more likely to experience feelings like anxiety or sadness. This also means you experience the world quite deeply.
neuroticism.lowBlurb: You tend to stay calm and emotionally steady even under pressure. You're rarely shaken by life's small ups and downs.

mbti.title: MBTI-style Personality Test
mbti.metaDescription: Discover which of the 16 personality types fits you best with this short, free test — right in your browser.

intro.heading: MBTI-style Personality Test
intro.body: This test uses 4 pairs of opposite personality tendencies — Extraversion/Introversion, Sensing/Intuition, Thinking/Feeling, Judging/Perceiving — to suggest which of the 16 personality types fits you best. It has 40 questions and takes about 6-8 minutes.
intro.disclaimer: This is a self-discovery tool for reference only, not a formal psychological assessment. Your results reflect tendencies, not a fixed label.

INTJ.name: The Planner
INTJ.blurb: You tend to think strategically, work independently, and stay focused on long-term goals. You'd rather find your own optimal solution than follow the crowd.

INTP.name: The Thinker
INTP.blurb: You tend to be curious about how things work, enjoy analyzing ideas logically, and prefer working independently.

ENTJ.name: The Leader
ENTJ.blurb: You tend to be decisive, enjoy organizing things efficiently, and naturally guide others toward a shared goal.

ENTP.name: The Debater
ENTP.blurb: You tend to enjoy debating ideas, thinking creatively, and constantly seeking new possibilities.

INFJ.name: The Empath
INFJ.blurb: You tend to be thoughtful, idealistic, and drawn to the deeper meaning behind things.

INFP.name: The Idealist
INFP.blurb: You tend to live by your personal values, feel things deeply, and always seek authenticity.

ENFJ.name: The Motivator
ENFJ.blurb: You tend to care about others, connect easily, and inspire the people around you.

ENFP.name: The Enthusiast
ENFP.blurb: You tend to be enthusiastic, creative, and love exploring new possibilities in life.

ISTJ.name: The Realist
ISTJ.blurb: You tend to be reliable, responsible, and work according to clear principles.

ISFJ.name: The Devoted One
ISFJ.blurb: You tend to be thoughtful, patient, and always ready to support the people around you.

ESTJ.name: The Organizer
ESTJ.blurb: You tend to be practical, organized, and prefer things to go according to plan.

ESFJ.name: The Caregiver
ESFJ.blurb: You tend to be sociable, considerate, and work hard to keep harmony within a group.

ISTP.name: The Practitioner
ISTP.blurb: You tend to be hands-on, enjoy solving problems yourself, and adapt quickly to new situations.

ISFP.name: The Artist
ISFP.blurb: You tend to be sensitive, drawn to beauty, and express yourself in your own way.

ESTP.name: The Doer
ESTP.blurb: You tend to be energetic, enjoy hands-on experiences, and react quickly to change.

ESFP.name: The Performer
ESFP.blurb: You tend to be cheerful, enjoy being the center of attention, and live in the moment.

enneagram.title: Enneagram Test
enneagram.metaDescription: Discover which of the 9 Enneagram types fits you best — free, right in your browser, no sign-up needed.

intro.heading: Enneagram Test
intro.body: The Enneagram is a 9-type model describing the deeper motivations behind how people behave and see the world. This test has 45 questions and takes about 6-8 minutes.
intro.disclaimer: This is a self-discovery tool for reference only, not a formal psychological assessment. Your results reflect tendencies, not a fixed label.

type1.name: The Reformer
type1.blurb: You tend to be principled, exacting, and always want to do things the right way.

type2.name: The Helper
type2.blurb: You tend to be warm, thoughtful, and find meaning in helping others.

type3.name: The Achiever
type3.blurb: You tend to be ambitious, image-conscious, and always working toward success.

type4.name: The Individualist
type4.blurb: You tend to be emotionally rich, value authenticity, and want to express your own identity.

type5.name: The Investigator
type5.blurb: You tend to be independent, enjoy deep learning, and need your own private space.

type6.name: The Loyalist
type6.blurb: You tend to be cautious, value security, and stay committed to what you trust.

type7.name: The Enthusiast
type7.blurb: You tend to be optimistic, love new experiences, and avoid heavy emotions.

type8.name: The Challenger
type8.blurb: You tend to be strong, decisive, and fiercely protective of the people you care about.

type9.name: The Peacemaker
type9.blurb: You tend to be calm, easygoing, and always looking to keep the peace around you.

items.item1: I always try to do things the best, most correct way possible.
items.item2: I notice and feel bothered when something isn't perfect.
items.item3: I hold high standards for myself and others.

disc.title: DISC Test
disc.metaDescription: Discover your behavioral style with the DISC model (Dominance, Influence, Steadiness, Conscientiousness) — free, right in your browser.

intro.heading: DISC Test
intro.body: DISC is a popular model describing behavioral style across 4 groups: Dominance (D), Influence (I), Steadiness (S), and Conscientiousness (C). This test has 28 questions and takes about 5 minutes.
intro.disclaimer: This is a self-discovery tool for reference only, not a formal psychological assessment. Your results reflect tendencies, not a fixed label.

D.name: Dominance (D)
D.highBlurb: You tend to be decisive, like being in control of a situation, and face challenges head-on.
D.lowBlurb: You tend to be cautious, preferring to think things through rather than take charge forcefully.

I.name: Influence (I)
I.highBlurb: You tend to be outgoing, energetic, and quick to connect with and inspire others.
I.lowBlurb: You tend to be reserved, preferring deep connections over wide-reaching socializing.

S.name: Steadiness (S)
S.highBlurb: You tend to be patient, reliable, and value stability and harmony.
S.lowBlurb: You tend to prefer change and a fast pace over familiar routines.

C.name: Conscientiousness (C)
C.highBlurb: You tend to be meticulous, principled, and value accuracy in your work.
C.lowBlurb: You tend to be flexible and easygoing rather than bound by strict processes.

items.item1: I like making decisions quickly and decisively.

items.item3: I prefer being in control of a situation rather than letting others lead.

loveLanguages.title: Love Languages Test
loveLanguages.metaDescription: Discover your love language among 5 categories — free, right in your browser, no sign-up needed.

intro.heading: Love Languages Test
intro.body: Love languages describe how each person most clearly feels and expresses affection, across 5 categories: Words of Affirmation, Quality Time, Receiving Gifts, Acts of Service, and Physical Touch. This test has 25 questions and takes about 4-5 minutes.
intro.disclaimer: This is a self-discovery tool for reference only, not a formal psychological assessment. Your results reflect tendencies, not a fixed label.

wordsOfAffirmation.name: Words of Affirmation
wordsOfAffirmation.blurb: You feel loved most clearly through words — sincere compliments, encouragement, and heartfelt expressions mean a lot to you.

qualityTime.name: Quality Time
qualityTime.blurb: You feel loved most clearly when someone gives you their full attention and time.

receivingGifts.name: Receiving Gifts
receivingGifts.blurb: You feel loved most clearly through thoughtful gifts, however small — what matters is the care behind them.

actsOfService.name: Acts of Service
actsOfService.blurb: You feel loved most clearly through concrete actions, when someone steps in to help and share the load with you.

physicalTouch.name: Physical Touch
physicalTouch.blurb: You feel loved most clearly through physical closeness, like a hug or holding hands.

items.item1: I feel loved when I hear sincere compliments.
items.item2: A well-timed word of encouragement can make my whole day better.
items.item3: I like it when someone close to me clearly says they appreciate me.
items.item4: I remember kind words people have said to me for a long time.
items.item5: I feel more connected when someone thanks me or says they're proud of me.
items.item6: I feel loved when someone gives me their full, undistracted attention.
items.item7: I prefer deep conversations over material gifts.
items.item8: I feel closer to someone when we do an activity together.
items.item9: I feel sad when someone important to me is always too busy for me.
items.item10: I treasure moments alone with someone, with no phones or distractions.
items.item11: I'm touched when I receive a gift, even a small one, that was thoughtfully chosen.
items.item12: I like keeping gifts as mementos.
items.item13: I feel cared for when someone remembers my preferences when picking a gift.
items.item14: I value the thoughtfulness behind a gift more than its price.
items.item15: I like giving gifts to others to show I care.
items.item16: I feel loved when someone proactively helps me with chores or errands.
items.item17: I appreciate it when people take action instead of just saying loving words.
items.item18: I feel cared for when someone takes care of something before I even get to it.
items.item19: I show affection by helping others with everyday tasks.
items.item20: I feel looked after when someone steps in to share my workload.
items.item21: I feel at ease when I'm hugged or holding hands with someone I love.
items.item22: A pat on the back or a light touch can make me feel connected.
items.item23: I express affection through physical gestures like holding hands or hugging.
items.item24: I feel disconnected if I go too long without physical closeness.
items.item25: I like sitting close to or leaning on someone I care about.

results.heading: Your results
results.resultDisclaimer: These are relative tendencies, not a fixed label. People can change over time and across situations.

attachmentStyle.title: Attachment Style Test
attachmentStyle.metaDescription: Discover your attachment style in relationships — free, right in your browser, no sign-up needed.

intro.heading: Attachment Style Test
intro.body: Attachment style describes how each person connects and reacts in close relationships, across 4 groups: Secure, Anxious, Avoidant, and Fearful-Avoidant. This test has 24 questions and takes about 4-5 minutes.
intro.disclaimer: This is a self-discovery tool for reference only, not a formal psychological assessment. Your results reflect tendencies, not a fixed label.

secure.name: Secure
secure.blurb: You tend to feel comfortable with closeness, trust others easily, and maintain stable, healthy relationships.

anxious.name: Anxious
anxious.blurb: You tend to crave closeness but also worry easily about being abandoned or not loved enough.

avoidant.name: Avoidant
avoidant.blurb: You tend to value independence and can feel suffocated when a relationship gets too close.

fearful.name: Fearful-Avoidant
fearful.blurb: You tend to want intimacy while also fearing getting hurt, which makes your feelings in relationships fluctuate.

items.item1: I feel comfortable being close and intimate with someone I love.
items.item2: I find it easy to trust my partner or the people close to me.
items.item3: I can share my true feelings without fear of being judged.
items.item4: I feel secure even when there's disagreement in a relationship.
items.item5: I trust that the people I love will be there when I need them.
items.item6: I can balance closeness with personal space in a relationship.
items.item7: I often worry that my partner will stop loving me.
items.item8: I need frequent reassurance that I'm loved.
items.item9: I feel anxious when my partner doesn't reply to messages right away.
items.item10: I tend to assume the worst when the other person seems distant.
items.item11: I'm afraid of being abandoned in close relationships.
items.item12: I think about my relationship often, even when things are fine.
items.item13: I prefer to stay independent rather than rely on someone else.
items.item14: I feel suffocated when a relationship gets too close.
items.item15: I find it hard to share my deeper feelings with others.
items.item16: I prefer solving problems myself rather than asking my partner for help.
items.item17: I tend to keep emotional distance even in close relationships.
items.item18: I feel uncomfortable when someone wants to know too much about my feelings.
items.item19: I want closeness but also fear getting hurt in a relationship.
items.item20: I find it hard to fully trust others, even though I want to be loved.
items.item21: My feelings in a relationship often swing between wanting closeness and pulling away.
items.item22: I sometimes push people away first because I'm afraid of getting hurt.
items.item23: I feel torn between wanting intimacy and fearing abandonment.
items.item24: I've been hurt before, so now I'm very cautious about opening up.

results.heading: Your results
results.resultDisclaimer: These are relative tendencies, not a fixed label. People can change over time and across situations.

phq9.title: Depression Screening (PHQ-9)
phq9.metaDescription: PHQ-9 depression screening — a free reference tool, right in your browser, your answers are never stored.

intro.heading: Depression Screening (PHQ-9)
intro.body: PHQ-9 is a widely used depression screening tool with 9 questions about what you've experienced over the past 2 weeks. It takes about 3 minutes.
intro.disclaimer: This is not a medical diagnostic tool — only a healthcare professional can provide an official diagnosis.

disclaimerGate.heading: Before you start
disclaimerGate.body: This is a self-screening tool based on the PHQ-9 scale. It does not replace a diagnosis from a doctor or mental health professional. Results are for reference only. If you're in crisis or having thoughts of harming yourself, please contact the Ngày Mai hotline (096 306 1414) right away or reach out to someone you trust.

items.item1: Little interest or pleasure in doing things
items.item3: Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much
items.item4: Feeling tired or having little energy
items.item6: Feeling bad about yourself — or that you are a failure, or have let yourself or your family down
items.item7: Trouble concentrating on things, such as reading the newspaper or watching television
items.item8: Moving or speaking so slowly that other people could have noticed — or the opposite, being so fidgety or restless that you've been moving around a lot more than usual
items.item9: Thoughts that you would be better off dead, or of hurting yourself in some way

minimal.label: Minimal
minimal.blurb: Your score shows very few related signs over the past 2 weeks. This is a reference tool only, not a medical conclusion.

mild.label: Mild
mild.blurb: Your score is in the mild range. If these feelings continue, consider talking to someone you trust or a mental health professional.

moderate.label: Moderate
moderate.blurb: Your score is in the moderate range — this can be a sign that talking to a mental health professional would help.

moderatelySevere.label: Moderately severe
moderatelySevere.blurb: Your score is fairly high. We encourage you to reach out to a mental health professional soon.

severe.label: Severe
severe.blurb: Your score is high — this is a clear sign you should talk to a mental health professional as soon as possible. You don't have to go through this alone.

results.heading: Your results
results.resultDisclaimer: This is a self-screening tool for reference only, not a medical diagnostic tool. Only a healthcare professional can provide an official diagnosis.
results.supportHeading: You don't have to do this alone
results.supportBody: If you'd like to talk to someone, the Ngày Mai hotline (096 306 1414) is available to listen, free and confidential.

crisis.heading: You're not alone
crisis.body: Some of your answers suggest you may be going through a very difficult time. Before seeing your results, please take a moment to look at the support resources below — someone is ready to listen.
crisis.ackCta: I've read this, show my results

gad7.title: Anxiety Screening (GAD-7)
gad7.metaDescription: GAD-7 anxiety screening — a free reference tool, right in your browser, your answers are never stored.

intro.heading: Anxiety Screening (GAD-7)
intro.body: GAD-7 is a widely used anxiety screening tool with 7 questions about what you've experienced over the past 2 weeks. It takes about 2 minutes.
intro.disclaimer: This is not a medical diagnostic tool — only a healthcare professional can provide an official diagnosis.

disclaimerGate.heading: Before you start
disclaimerGate.body: This is a self-screening tool based on the GAD-7 scale. It does not replace a diagnosis from a doctor or mental health professional. Results are for reference only. If you're in crisis, please contact the Ngày Mai hotline (096 306 1414) right away or reach out to someone you trust.

items.item1: Feeling nervous, anxious, or on edge
items.item2: Not being able to stop or control worrying
items.item3: Worrying too much about different things
items.item5: Being so restless that it's hard to sit still
items.item7: Feeling afraid as if something awful might happen

minimal.label: Minimal
minimal.blurb: Your score shows very few signs of anxiety over the past 2 weeks. This is a reference tool only, not a medical conclusion.

mild.label: Mild
mild.blurb: Your score is in the mild range. If the worrying continues, consider talking to someone you trust or a mental health professional.

moderate.label: Moderate
moderate.blurb: Your score is in the moderate range — this can be a sign that talking to a mental health professional would help.

severe.label: Severe
severe.blurb: Your score is high. We encourage you to reach out to a mental health professional soon.

results.heading: Your results
results.resultDisclaimer: This is a self-screening tool for reference only, not a medical diagnostic tool. Only a healthcare professional can provide an official diagnosis.
results.supportHeading: You don't have to do this alone
results.supportBody: If you'd like to talk to someone, the Ngày Mai hotline (096 306 1414) is available to listen, free and confidential.
